# User Utility vs Platform Proxy Model

## Purpose
This separates what a platform measures from what a user actually values.

User true utility:

\[
U_{u,i}=Q_{u,i}-C_{u,i}
\]

Platform proxy reward:

\[
Y_{u,i}=\alpha \cdot watch + \beta \cdot like + \gamma \cdot comment + \delta \cdot share
\]

Manufactured traction exploits the gap:

\[
Y_{u,i}\uparrow \quad \text{while} \quad U_{u,i}\not\uparrow
\]

## Examples
Rage bait may increase comments but reduce welfare. Fake reviews may increase purchases while reducing post-purchase satisfaction. Dark patterns may increase subscriptions while decreasing consumer autonomy.
